description: Next.js cache poisoning chains (Allam 2025, PortSwigger). ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration) + RSC cache key manipulation + Server Action body confusion. Affects 13.x-15.x App Router.

rsc_cache_key_manipulation.description: RSC responses keyed on URL + cookie subset. Inject headers that the cache strips but origin reflects — poisoned response served to subsequent users.

isr_revalidate_poison.description: On-demand revalidation tokens — if revalidate endpoint is unauthenticated, attacker forces stale-content replacement.

probes.description: Revalidation endpoint fingerprint — common Next.js handler pattern

server_action_body_confusion.description: Server Action POST body cached because handler returns 200 + cacheable headers. Subsequent users see attacker action result.

probes.description: Detect Server Action that returns cacheable response (header anomaly)
